title: Audit vendor.js for INP-blocking code — INP 280ms target ~200ms
description: Phase 2 priority #2. INP p75 is 280ms (AVG band, want <200ms FAST). Likely candidate is heavy vendor.js work on first interaction. Profile via Chrome DevTools Performance tab on first tap interaction; identify long tasks; split or defer where possible. Lower-stakes than CLS/LCP but moves a Core Web Vital that affects ranking.
notes:
2026-04-29 production INP attribution from GA4 RUM (last 7d, 7,395 INP events):

PAGE BREAKDOWN:
  / (homepage)                                503 events  67.0% good  13.1% poor  ← WORST
  /pages/azta-urban-salon-service-menu        315 events  82.9% good   7.3% poor
  /pages/search-results-page                  626 events  91.4% good   3.8% poor
  /collections/best-sellers                    51 events  74.5% good   9.8% poor

DEBUG_TARGET FOR POOR INPS (>500ms):
  (not set)                                   345 events  100% poor   ← 97% of all poor
  #MainContent                                  3 events  100% poor
  #pwa-modal                                    3 events  100% poor   (residual; CSS contained, was 89%)
  div.input-group...input-group-                7 needs-improv  (search input)
  span.swiper-pagination-bullet                 1 poor  (Swiper pagination tap)

ROOT CAUSE:
The (not set) signature for 97% of poor INPs means the slow interaction wasn't a click on a specific themed element — it's main-thread JS work executing at page-load time blocking the user's first scroll/tap. This is TBT-bound, not handler-bound.

CONCRETE THEME-SIDE FINDING:
Homepage initializes 8 Swiper instances at DOMContentLoaded (7 .swiper.collection + 1 banner-slider). Custom-theme.js line 14292 querySelectorAll(".swiper.collection") + new Swiper() for each. Each Swiper does:
  - Layout measurement (clientWidth × N slides)
  - Touch + pointer + keyboard listener binding
  - Initial slide positioning

With 315 swiper-slides on the page and 8 instances initializing serially, this is significant main-thread work — exactly when the user's first interaction happens. Filed beads-???? for lazy-init via IntersectionObserver (medium-risk because requires wrapping bundled custom-theme.js code).

GLOBAL CLICK LISTENERS AUDITED:
- theme.js: 1 body click (Popout.bodyClick — fires only for visible popouts; cheap)
- custom-theme.js: 1 body click (sweetalert2.bindClickHandler — never actually invoked by theme code, dead listener attachment surface but not active)
- vendor.js: 1 window click + 1 doc keydown
- inline theme.liquid: data-drawer-toggle delegated click (cheap, early-return if no match)

None of these are individually slow enough to cause 200ms+ INP.

WHAT WOULD MOVE INP NOW:
1. shop.js defer (just shipped 2026-04-29, ~1-1.5s TBT estimated win on Slow 4G)
2. Lazy-init below-fold Swipers (filed as new beads task)
3. Vendor.js tree-shake (remove ellipsis ~2KB; conditional Rellax ~10KB; axios→fetch ~8KB) — requires build-pipeline access not in this repo

BLOCKED ON: actual PSI re-run after shop.js defer reaches CrUX (typically 7-28d roll-up) to validate INP improvement.

title: Add sizes attribute to product thumbnails — avoid mobile downloading 4100w variants
description: Product thumbnails in snippets/product-grid-item.liquid:140 lack a sizes attribute, so mobile browsers download desktop-size variants (up to 4100w) on collection pages. Add sizes='(min-width: 1024px) 25vw, (min-width: 640px) 33vw, 50vw' or similar matching the responsive grid. Expected: LCP -0.5s on mobile collection pages.
close_reason: Already shipped in earlier work — verified via grep on snippets/product-grid-item.liquid. The primary tags at lines 174, 202, 226 all have sizes='(min-width: 1024px) 25vw, (min-width: 640px) 33vw, 50vw'. Mobile correctly picks 360w/540w variants instead of 1080w+. Only the noscript fallback at line 242 lacks sizes — irrelevant since noscript only fires when JS is fully disabled. No new code needed; closing as already-done.

title: Fix CLS 0.30 — homepage hover-state width/height redraw on .double__image
description: Top single fix in Phase 2 perf priorities — CLS p75 0.30 is 3x the POOR threshold. Root cause: hover-state width/height redraw on .double__image:hover .collection__image__bottom (assets/theme.css:14679). Remove the redundant width/height from the hover state so hover does not trigger a layout-shifting redraw. Expected: CLS 0.30 → ~0.10.
close_reason: Already fixed in commit 3c381e9 (2026-04-26 'Reconcile working tree with live theme'). Hover state at assets/theme.css:12222 no longer declares width/height — only opacity/visibility/transition. Inline comment documents the CLS fix (-0.05 lab) + INP improvement. Roadmap reference to line 14679 was stale (file shifted with subsequent CSS extractions). Field CLS p75 0.30 has additional contributors beyond this one hover redraw — investigate via GA4 web-vitals attribution data once 24-48h flows (filing follow-up issue).

title: LimeSpot widget admin tweaks (4 fixes, free, no code)
description: Audit on 2026-04-26 found 4 zero-code wins. (1) Cart page has 0 widgets — biggest miss; cart-page widget gives 2-5% AOV uplift in beauty. Enable in LimeSpot admin → Widgets. (2) PDP widgets show 18+20=38 products; reduce to 6-8 per widget (Baymard/NN/g best practice). (3) PDP widgets stacked at 32-38% scroll depth; place FBT under Add-to-Cart, YMAL further down. (4) Homepage has 0 widgets; add Trending or Bestsellers. Same backend, free.

title: Sunset Shop Sheriff AMP: wait 48h → GMC verify mobile_link gone → uninstall
description: Saves $1,188/yr. Step 1 done 2026-04-26 (removed shop-sheriff-amp include from theme.liquid). Remaining: wait 48h after April 26 commit so Google re-crawls. Then in GMC: open any product → Additional details → confirm mobile_link gone or matches canonical. Then uninstall Shop Sheriff AMP from Shopify admin → Apps. Test redirect: visit /a/s/products/davines-... should 301 to /products/davines-... If 404s, contact Shop Sheriff support for redirect setup. Monitor GSC for 30d.

title: Delete orphan snippets/judgeme_widgets copy.liquid (4.3KB dead file)
description: Found during Judge.me branch review. File is a copy of judgeme_widgets.liquid that does not appear to be rendered anywhere. Still uses old jdgm-preview-badge class. Hygiene cleanup; out of scope for the cherry-pick commit but flag for separate removal.
close_reason: Local file deleted in commit (was 4.3 KB dead snippet, duplicate of judgeme_widgets.liquid with old class name). File may still exist on live + draft themes; admin delete via Shopify admin → Edit code → Snippets path. Same pattern as bd nra (BSS asset cleanup).

title: Push Judge.me defer to draft theme 140785582179 + DevTools/PSI verification
description: After cherry-pick + click-to-scroll fix, push 5 Judge.me files to draft theme. Verify on draft preview URL: (a) /collections/all has zero loader.js requests in Network tab, (b) /pages/reviews loads loader.js when widget intersects, (c) static stars on PDP click → scroll to reviews works, (d) side-by-side eyeball draft vs live for star alignment + color, (e) PSI on draft collection URL vs live, expect mobile LCP win.
close_reason: Shipped to live theme 131664707683 on 2026-04-27. Verified live HTML: 142 jm-stars-static elements rendered (matching homepage product count), 0 OLD .jdgm-preview-badge wrappers, 363 NEW .jdgm-prev-badge wrappers, 677/24/11 on/half/off star distribution. Half-star + count-text + click-to-scroll all confirmed. Real wins live: bypass widget/main.js download (loader.js can't match renamed wrappers), server-rendered stars paint with the card.

title: Add click-to-scroll wrapper to Judge.me static stars (PDP only)
description: Original Judge.me hydrated badge wraps stars in a clickable element bound to scroll to #judgeme_product_reviews. Branch's static stars have no click target — UX regression on PDP. Fix in snippets/judgeme_static_stars.liquid: accept optional scroll_target param, wrap in <a href> when set. Pass scroll_target='#judgeme_product_reviews' from judgeme_widgets.liquid only when called from PDP context (not collection cards).
close_reason: Implemented in commit 68f41fb. judgeme_static_stars.liquid accepts optional scroll_target param + conditionally wraps stars in <a>. judgeme_widgets.liquid passes '#judgeme_product_reviews' only when template == 'product'. Preserves Judge.me's original click-to-scroll UX on PDP including related-product carousels (matches loader.js behavior of binding same handler to all .jdgm-prev-badge on page). Pushed to draft theme 140785582179.

title: Verify Judge.me loader.js queries .jdgm-prev-badge vs .jdgm-preview-badge
description: Branch renames wrapper class from jdgm-preview-badge to jdgm-prev-badge. Open hairmnl.com/products/<any-product-with-reviews> in DevTools, find loader.js from cdn.judge.me, search source for both prev-badge and preview-badge. Whichever appears as querySelector argument is what loader hydrates. If loader queries the full word, click-to-scroll regression is permanent unless we add href wrapper. Blocks merge decision.
close_reason: Verified by fetching loader.js v490 from cdn.shopify.com/extensions/.../judgeme-490/assets/loader.js. Loader maps widget files to gating selectors; widget/main.js (the badge hydration code) is gated on '.jdgm-preview-badge, .judgeme-preview-badge' (full word, both spellings). Branch's renamed '.jdgm-prev-badge' (short) wrappers will NOT match — widget/main.js never downloads on pages with only preview badges. Bonus: even on PDPs with review widgets where loader fires for other selectors, the badge hydration JS is skipped. No race, no flicker, no double-render. Click-to-scroll regression is permanent (loader handler bound to .jdgm-preview-badge can't find renamed wrappers) — exactly what 68f41fb proactively fixed via <a href> wrapper.

title: Cherry-pick Judge.me defer commit 1849c56 onto fresh branch off main
description: Branch claude/connect-desktop-session-lF8AX is 66 commits behind main. Cherry-pick the single commit 1849c56 onto a new branch perf/judgeme-defer cut from current main. Expect a conflict on snippets/critical-css.liquid (main now has minified single-line version; branch authored against multi-line). Manually port the 5 new static-stars CSS rules to end of mains minified file. Files in commit: snippets/judgeme_static_stars.liquid (new), snippets/judgeme_widgets.liquid, snippets/judgeme_core.liquid, snippets/critical-css.liquid, snippets/pro-blogger.snippet.related-item-rating.liquid.
close_reason: Cherry-picked 1849c56 onto perf/judgeme-defer branch off main; resolved critical-css.liquid conflict by appending 5 new static-stars rules inline to main's minified version. Commit facc161. 4 files cleanly merged, only critical-css needed manual resolution.

title: Phase 0: CRO data layer foundation (Clarity + PostHog + GrowthBook + BigQuery + consent gate)
description: Umbrella for the 8-task Phase 0 setup defined in /Users/y9378348c/Downloads/HairMNL-CRO-DataLayer-Proposal.md. Defaults confirmed by user 2026-04-29 EOD: self-host OK (Hetzner CX11 ~$5/mo), no existing CMP (add CookieYes free tier), A/B winner workflow human-review for first 5 then auto-promote, enable GA4→BigQuery export as Step 0, perf regression cap ≤200ms TBT hard threshold. Tasks: (1) provision Hetzner VPS, (2) deploy PostHog via Docker compose, (3) deploy GrowthBook on same VPS, (4) enable GA4→BigQuery daily export, (5) sign up Microsoft Clarity + configure PII masking, (6) install CookieYes consent banner, (7) wire Clarity+PostHog scripts behind consent gate with lazy-load after LCP, (8) verify perf threshold via pre/post PWI. Total est ~6 hrs dev. Cost: $5/mo. Break down into sub-tasks when starting Phase 0 work.

title: Lazy-init below-fold Swipers on homepage via IntersectionObserver
description:
Production INP attribution (RUM 2026-04-29) shows homepage at 67% good / 13.1% poor — worst on site. 97% of poor INP events have (not set) debug_target = page-load JS work blocking first interaction.

Homepage has 8 Swiper instances initializing at DOMContentLoaded:
  - 7 .swiper.collection (custom-theme.js:14292)
  - 1 banner-slider (separate init)
  - 315 .swiper-slide elements total

Each Swiper does layout measurement + touch/pointer/keyboard listener binding at init. Doing this for 8 swipers serially in one task is the most likely source of the (not set) INPs.

PROPOSED FIX:
Add a tiny IntersectionObserver wrapper that defers new Swiper(...) for below-fold .swiper.collection elements until they scroll into the viewport. The first slider (above fold) keeps eager init for visual polish; the other 6-7 init lazily.

Implementation options:
A. Modify the custom-theme.js bundle itself (requires source repo + build pipeline — likely not in this repo)
B. Add a wrapper snippet that runs BEFORE custom-theme.js on DOMContentLoaded, removing the .swiper.collection class from below-fold elements, then restoring + triggering the init manually when they intersect. Brittle but doable from theme code only.
C. Add a snippet that disables Swiper auto-init by removing .collection from class, then re-adds + manually calls Swiper constructor on intersect. Needs Swiper to be exposed globally — currently it's IIFE-bundled inside custom-theme.js.

RECOMMENDED: Option B with extensive testing. Estimated win: 200-400ms TBT reduction on homepage initial load = direct INP improvement (since INP is bounded by main-thread availability).

VALIDATION: Re-run web-vitals-report.py --metric INP --by page after 14d to confirm homepage INP %good moves from 67% toward 75% target.
close_reason: IntersectionObserver wrapper live on both draft and live theme. 6 of 7 .swiper.collection instances now lazy-init 200px before viewport entry.

title: Remove Snapchat Pixel (admin-side: Custom Pixel, app embed, or GTM)
description:
User flagged Snapchat analytics in Network tab (sc-static.net/scevent.min.js + tr.snapchat.com, ~26KB total). Theme-code grep finds ZERO references — pixel is not in any liquid/json/js file. Live HTML grep also returns zero. Pixel is loaded admin-side via one of:

1. Shopify Admin → Settings → Customer events → Custom pixels → find Snapchat-related custom pixel → Disconnect
2. Shopify Admin → Apps → find Snapchat Channel or any connected Snapchat app → Uninstall
3. Google Tag Manager (if installed) → tagmanager.google.com → find Snapchat-related tag → pause or delete
4. GA4 / GTM-bound third-party pixel manager

Need user (admin access) to identify which of the above is loading it and disable. Theme-side has nothing to remove. Once removed, verify via Network tab: no requests to sc-static.net or tr.snapchat.com.

title: Roll out forloop.first eager+fetchpriority pattern to other above-fold sections
description:
Today shipped the LCP fix on section-gallery.liquid (forloop.first → eager + fetchpriority=high). Same pattern should apply to other sections that render above-the-fold images via image-fill snippet:

- section-hero.liquid — homepage hero, single image, always above fold → eager+priority
- collection-hero.liquid — top of collection page, always above fold → eager+priority
- snippets/hero.liquid — wrapper used by multiple sections → check usage pattern
- section-banner-slider.liquid — homepage banner slider, FIRST slide above fold → eager+priority on first slide
- snippets/article-grid-item.liquid — blog article grid, FIRST card above fold → eager on first
- snippets/collection-grid-item.liquid — same as above for collection grids
- sections/section-video.liquid — first video poster image above fold → eager

Approx time: 1-2 hr to update all 7 sections + verify each via PSI's lcp-discovery-insight on representative page types.

Why P2: this directly attacks the 1.41K mobile URLs flagged in GSC for LCP > 2.5s. Each section fixed = a class of pages improved.

title: Blog template CLS — secondary culprits beyond #pwa-modal (low-porosity 0.54, best-hair-color 0.53)
description:
GSC CrUX field data shows TWO blog pages with CLS in 0.50+ range (>2x the poor threshold of 0.25):
- /blogs/hair-education/low-porosity-hair-what-is-it-and-what-should-you-do-about-it: CLS 0.54 (135 affected)
- /blogs/salon-services/best-hair-color-for-morenas-complement-your-natural-glow: CLS 0.53 (158 affected)

GA4 RUM debug_target attribution on these pages showed:
- 55 events: #pwa-modal (PRIMARY, addressed by separate CSS fix)
- 1 event: #shopify-section-footer (footer late-render, minor)

But 0.54 CLS is more than just #pwa-modal — there must be additional shifts not captured by the dominant attribution. Likely candidates on blog template:
1. AOS scroll animations (data-aos elements might shift)
2. Bloggle's own lazysizes loader (sections/main-bloggle-article.liquid:358 — different lazysizes than ours)
3. Long-form text reflow as web fonts load (font-display: swap on body fonts)
4. Inline blog images without explicit width/height
5. Social share buttons / table of contents widgets

Investigation needed:
1. Open one of the blog URLs in Chrome DevTools → Performance → record load → identify all CLS shift sources
2. Verify that #pwa-modal CSS fix (containment) gets blogs from 0.54 → ~0.20-0.30 (still poor but reduced)
3. Address remaining shifts per filmstrip

Worth a 2-3 hr focused session AFTER #pwa-modal fix lands and we re-measure.
close_reason: Substantially resolved by #pwa-modal containment fix (commit 29e0f3c). Blog page low-porosity-hair CLS dropped 0.54 → 0.030 — well within 'good' threshold (<0.1). Remaining tiny shifts (0.015 inline blog img + 0.008 bta-reservation widget + 0.006 lion-notification) sum to 0.030 total — not worth dedicated work. Re-open if CrUX field data shows blog CLS staying poor after 28-day refresh window.

title: Theme-check linter pass: 1,562 offenses across 163 files (171 errors, 1391 warnings)
description:
2026-04-28 ran 'shopify theme check' on the entire theme. Comprehensive findings:

By severity:
  - 171 errors
  - 1391 warnings

Top checks (issue types):
  848 VariableName — naming convention warnings (cosmetic)
  206 DeprecatedFilter — img_url -> image_url (bulk find-replace, low risk if done carefully)
  151 UnusedAssign — unused {% assign %} vars (cosmetic, may save bytes)
   81 DeprecatedTag — old Liquid tags
   50 MatchingTranslations — locale file mismatches
   44 RemoteAsset — assets loaded from external URLs (could self-host)
   38 LiquidHTMLSyntaxError — mostly false positives in svg.liquid + js.liquid
   34 MissingTemplate — broken {% render %} calls (real bugs, see below)
   32 ImgWidthAndHeight — img tags missing width/height (CLS impact, see e4a)
   29 UndefinedObject — references to undefined Liquid objects

FIXED in 2026-04-28 commit (3 small fixes from this audit):
  - snippets/pro-blogger.snippet.related-item.liquid:23,48 — 2x /cart/add hardcoded → routes.cart_add_url
  - snippets/collections-sidebar.liquid:74,92,117 — /collections/all hardcoded → routes.all_products_collection_url
  - snippets/collections-sidebar.liquid:79 — /collections/{handle} hardcoded → routes.collections_url

CRITICAL BUG found, NOT YET FIXED:
  layout/theme.liquid:676 has 6 broken {% render %} calls with double-extension paths:
    {% render 'bss-passcode-to-see-price-js.liquid' %}  (should be 'bss-passcode-to-see-price-js')
    {% render 'bsscommerce-login-to-see-price-custom-js.liquid' %}
    {% render 'bss-ltap-fl-redirect.liquid' %}
  These render calls FAIL SILENTLY (the {% unless contains 'Liquid error' %} swallows the error).
  Effect: BSS B2B 'login to see price' + 'passcode to see price' + 'first-login redirect' features are non-functional.
  Decision needed: are these BSS features supposed to work? If yes, fix the render paths. If no, delete the dead code.
  See beads bjk for the broader BSS B2B uninstall decision.

OTHER MissingTemplate refs (all dead-app leftovers — likely safe to delete after verifying app uninstall):
  - icon-error.liquid (referenced 6x by various BSS B2B passcode forms)
  - ly-global.liquid (referenced by pluginseo-langify — Langify language app)
  - wof_product_limit, wlm-search, wcp_discount, wcp_variant, wcp_vd_product, wlm-product (referenced by search.wof/collection.wof — WishList Plus app?)
  - section-static-banner.liquid (judgeme_allReviewsPage)
  - bss-ltap-custom-passcode-form.liquid
  - sections/collection-lp-serioxyl.liquid (template references missing section)

MissingAsset refs (8):
  - assets/sca.affiliate.refer-customer-data.js (SCA affiliate app)
  - assets/filename.png (mbc-bundles — placeholder bug?)
  - assets/nil.jpg (3 references — likely intentional placeholder pattern)
  - assets/ico-select.svg, cursor-zoom.svg, cursor-zoom-2x.svg (in css-variables.liquid)

Worth a dedicated 4-6 hour clean-up sprint. Most impactful fixes:
1. Fix or delete the 6 broken BSS renders in theme.liquid:676 (real broken feature)
2. Address 32 ImgWidthAndHeight (CLS-positive)
3. Bulk img_url → image_url migration (modernization, allows format:'webp', etc)
4. Audit 44 RemoteAsset for self-hosting candidates

title: davines-momo-shampoo PDP returns 404 for guest visitors (BSS B2B redirect)
description:
Discovered 2026-04-28: navigating to /products/davines-momo-shampoo as a guest browser (no BSS B2B cookies set) returns the 404 page instead of the product. This affects:

1. PSI testing — PSI lab user has no cookies, so all PSI runs of this URL test the 404 page, NOT the actual PDP. The CLS/LCP numbers we've been quoting for 'momo' are for the 404 page (which has a Judge.me medals widget at the bottom contributing CLS).
2. Real shoppers — guests landing on this product from Google search/etc presumably also see 404. SEO impact + lost conversion.
3. This product is presumably restricted via BSS B2B Customer Portal app's 'Hide product' rules.

Traced to sections/product.liquid:1-4 — bsscommerce-redirect-product-page-logic snippet runs server-side, redirects unauthenticated guests away.

Need founder/web-team decision:
1. Is davines-momo INTENTIONALLY B2B-only? If yes, set noindex robots tag on the redirect target so Google doesn't surface it.
2. If unintentional, fix the BSS rules to allow guest access.
3. If it's a phased/staged rollout, document which products are restricted and ensure SEO accordingly.

Quick check: 'bd show hairmnl-theme-bjk' (existing BSS B2B uninstall decision ticket) — full BSS uninstall would remove this whole class of issue.

title: PDP residual CLS — late-loading widgets shift <main> + <footer> (~0.42 + 0.16)
description:
After the 2026-04-28 cap-removal fix on --default-ratio (commit 3cfa9b9), the Flickity-init column shift on PDPs is gone. davines-momo CLS dropped from 0.600 to 0.421 (median of 3). But residual ~0.42 shift on <main> and ~0.16 on <footer> remains.

These page-level shifts are caused by late-loading third-party widgets injecting content above-the-fold or pushing the main content area:
- Klaviyo signup modal (visible in PSI filmstrip frame 8 on serioxyl)
- LoyaltyLion notification ('Sign up for HairMNL Rewards to get a Php100 voucher')
- BSS B2B price hide injection (loading-prompt element on guest pageload)
- BOGOS / SCA free-gift element (.sca-gift-image present in PSI unsized-images audit)
- Reamaze chat widget (small shifts but cumulative)
- Cookie consent banner (visible in PSI filmstrip frame 5 on serioxyl)

Next investigation steps:
1. Use Lighthouse 'Avoid large layout shifts' diagnostic at the per-element level to identify the SPECIFIC widget causing the 0.42 main shift
2. Check if any of these widgets can be reserved-space (height pre-allocated via CSS) before they inject content
3. Klaviyo + LoyaltyLion specifically: their popup/notification timing — can we delay until after first paint stabilizes?
4. Cookie banner: where is it injected from? Should always reserve space.

Worth ~2-3hr of dedicated investigation. Likely needs Chrome DevTools Performance tab to see which JS triggers each widget injection.
notes:
2026-04-28 deep-dive: Lighthouse shift entries on momo "PDP" (which actually returns 404 to PSI's guest browser due to BSS B2B redirect at sections/product.liquid:1-4 — pre-existing issue) attribute the 0.421 shift to a Judge.me logo image LACKING EXPLICIT SIZE in the .jdgm-medals-wrapper / .jdgm-verified-by section. Subsequent main-shift cause: media element (Judge.me logo) loaded.

Real PDP test on /products/loreal-professionnel-serioxyl-advanced-densifying-shampoo (which renders properly): big shifts of 0.3628 + 0.3003 on grid__item.medium-up--one-half have NO LIGHTHOUSE-IDENTIFIED CAUSE. The audit details show only tiny shifts (0.0001) attributed to "Media element lacking an explicit size" (the product img with width=1180 height=X attrs that should give it aspect-ratio) and "Web font loaded".

Hypothesis for the unattributed 0.36+0.30 shifts:
1. Flickity init re-resize (already partially fixed by --default-ratio cap removal in commit 3cfa9b9, but only helps tall portrait images — square images still affected)
2. Multiple text reflows from web-font swap (font-display: swap on Playfair Display per snippets/custom-fonts.liquid). Fallback to custom font swap changes line heights → column reflows.
3. Description-column dynamic content (variant selector, BSS B2B price hide injection, store-pickup widget)

Theme-side mitigations to consider next:
- Add explicit width/height OR aspect-ratio on the Judge.me logo via custom CSS in css-overrides.liquid
- Switch font-display from "swap" to "optional" (eliminates FOUT but font may not appear on slow connections) OR add size-adjust/ascent-override on @font-face to match fallback metrics
- Use container queries or fixed min-height on description column blocks that JS-inject content
- Investigate via real-shopper data per beads 7fz (GA4 web-vitals attribution)

Branched out of P0 PDP CLS work. The cap-removal fix (3cfa9b9) did reduce momo's apparent CLS. Further reduction needs either field data (7fz) or browser-emulation testing (which Brave's resize_window doesn't actually shrink the viewport for, blocking direct mobile-viewport DevTools work).

title: Elevar /a/elevar tracking endpoint takes 2.6s on every PSI run
description:
2026-04-28 PSI diagnostic showed /a/elevar (Elevar's GTM-proxy / event-collection endpoint at the shop's own domain) consistently taking 2,634ms — the single biggest network-time hog on the homepage.

Elevar proxies tracking events through the shop's own domain (under /a/elevar) to bypass ad-blockers and 3rd-party-cookie restrictions. Slow response from this endpoint adds to TBT (because subsequent JS waits on the response) and overall page load time.

Diagnostic data (/tmp/psi-result.json from 2026-04-28 morning):
  - URL pattern: /a/elevar?source_url=...
  - Method: Fetch
  - Time: 2,634ms
  - Transfer size: 1KB

Next steps:
1. Open Elevar dashboard → check event-collection latency metrics. Compare HairMNL vs Elevar's average client.
2. Email Elevar support: 'Why is /a/elevar consistently 2.6s on PSI? Can we narrow this?' Include the diagnostic data.
3. Consider whether the Elevar proxy is necessary, or if direct GTM (without Elevar's proxy) would be faster. Trade-off: ad-blockers will block direct GTM more, losing tracking signal.
4. If Elevar can't speed up: defer the Elevar script load further, or fire events on requestIdleCallback only (not on page load).

Admin / vendor-side work, not theme code. ~30 min initial investigation.

title: Cloudflare bot challenge fires on rapid add-to-cart sequences (admin-side, not theme)
description:
Reported 2026-04-28 night: shoppers adding several items rapidly trigger Cloudflare's bot/rate-limit verification screen. Once challenged, /cart/add.js POSTs may be blocked or delayed, and theme.js asset loading may also be affected, leaving the cart drawer in a broken state.

This is NOT a theme code issue — Cloudflare is rate-limiting based on request volume per IP per window. The theme can mitigate the post-challenge UX (see hairmnl-theme-dc2 follow-up: drawer-toggle intercept rewritten to be self-contained, no longer depends on theme.js binding handlers), but cannot prevent the challenge itself.

Admin-side fixes to investigate (Shopify admin → Online Store → Domains → Cloudflare config, or via Cloudflare dashboard if account-managed):
1. Whitelist /cart/add.js, /cart/change.js, /cart.js endpoints from bot detection — these are AJAX endpoints that legitimate users hit several times per session.
2. Reduce 'Bot Fight Mode' or 'Under Attack Mode' sensitivity for those paths.
3. Set up a Page Rule that bypasses the WAF for these paths.
4. Consider increasing the rate-limit threshold or adjusting the window.

Theme-side mitigations already shipped today:
- Self-contained drawer-toggle intercept (so cart click still works even if theme.js loading is throttled)
- Liquid-side conditional render of cart message (no JS dependency for correct initial render)

Theme-side mitigations NOT shipped (would require build):
- Client-side throttle on add-to-cart button (e.g., disable for 500 ms after click). Risk: shoppers click multiple times thinking nothing happened, worsens UX.
- Optimistic UI updates that don't depend on /cart.js after each add. Possible but invasive.

Worth a 30-min admin session to check Cloudflare config.

title: Cart drawer dual-message bug: success + default both render when subtotal >= free-shipping threshold
description:
When cart subtotal qualifies for free shipping, the drawer's promo banner shows BOTH .cart__message-success ('Congratulations! Your order qualifies for free shipping') AND .cart__message-default ('Spend ₱-1,150 more...') simultaneously, concatenated as one long line. Expected: only .cart__message-success visible per cart.css line 43-45 rule: .cart__message.is-success .cart__message-success ~ .cart__message-default { display: none } Observed (2026-04-28 night, hotfix-confirmed-shipped state): both visible. Likely cause: the AJAX drawer-rerender path at theme.js:5205 (renderPopdown) replaces drawer.innerHTML with freshly-rendered Liquid fetched via ?section_id=api-product-popdown. The fresh fragment may render with class_message='' (cart state mismatch with Liquid render context) OR the freeShippingMessageHandle JS listener (theme.js:1737) doesn't re-fire after innerHTML swap. Diagnostic next steps: 1. Use Chrome MCP to inspect live DOM right after drawer opens — confirm whether parent

has is-success class 2. If is-success missing: check Liquid render context for the popdown section (cart state availability) 3. If is-success present: check for higher-specificity override or inline style 4. Consider robust fix: change cart-drawer.liquid lines 105-111 to conditionally render ONLY ONE span based on class_message, eliminating the CSS-toggle dependency. Note: this loses dynamic JS update; may need re-trigger of CartShippingMessage class on AJAX rerender.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 'fix(cart-drawer): kill 4 residual bugs surfaced by tonight's CSS hotfix'. All four shipped to live theme 131664707683 on 2026-04-28 night. Verified on draft 140785582179 before live push.

title: Resolve duplicate sale labels on product cards (product__badge--custom vs product-labels system)
description: Pre-existing tension found during Judge.me draft smoke test 2026-04-27. Some product cards (e.g., AVEDA Nutriplenish on /collections/aveda or homepage) render TWO sale labels when compare_at_price > price: (1) the original .product__badge.product__badge--custom red ribbon with text like '10% Off' (custom per-product badge from theme system, possibly metafield-driven), and (2) the new .product-label.product-label--sale auto-computed '-10%' from snippets/product-labels.liquid (added in commit 06fd1d8 to replace BSS Product Labels). Both fire when product is on sale. Decide: (a) remove the auto-computed sale label from product-labels.liquid (keeping it only for tag-based labels and Sold Out), letting the existing custom badge handle sale display; (b) remove the custom badge system entirely, migrate any per-product custom text to label:* tags, let product-labels handle everything; (c) keep both but visually de-duplicate via CSS so only one shows when both fire. Currently visible on draft theme 140785582179 only because draft was just synced; live theme renders only the custom badge because live's product-grid-item-branded.liquid is older and doesn't include the {% render 'product-labels' %} call.
notes: Code-traced 2026-04-27. RED label = .product__badge.product__badge--custom rendered by snippets/product-grid-item.liquid:69, content from per-product custom_badge_content variable (metafield/setting), free-form text set per product. Examples seen on draft homepage: '10% Off', 'free gift', 'free gifts', 'sale'. BEIGE label = .product-label.product-label--sale rendered by snippets/product-labels.liquid (called via {% render %} from product-grid-item-branded.liquid and product-grid-item-branded-subcollection.liquid), auto-computed -X% from compare_at_price > price math. RECOMMENDED RESOLUTION (option B): migrate non-sale custom badge text (free gift, etc.) to label:* product tags so the new product-labels.liquid system handles all labels (sale auto-computed, others tag-driven). Then remove product__badge--custom logic from product-grid-item.liquid. Single source of truth. ~30 min admin migration via Shopify bulk edit on tagged products.

title: Investigate top field-CLS shift targets via GA4 web-vitals attribution (real-shopper data)
description: Lab CLS today is 0.047 (GOOD) but field CrUX CLS p75 is 0.30 (POOR, 3x threshold). Lab/field divergence means CLS is happening post-load (interaction-driven, not initial paint). Specific fix in y89 (hover redraw) is shipped — accounts for ~0.05 of the 0.30. Remaining 0.25 needs attribution data we are already capturing. The web-vitals v4 reporter (snippets/web-vitals-reporter.liquid, shipped commit 367f920) sends largestShiftTarget per CLS event to GA4 with debug_target param. Once 24-48h of data flows: (1) GA4 → Engagement → Events → CLS → analyze top debug_target values; OR use Page 2 Table 3 of the perf dashboard (issue 24q includes this surfacing). (2) Identify top 3 selectors. (3) Fix each one. Common candidates to look for in the data: late-loading images without dimensions, font swap (FOUT), Klaviyo popup positioning, ad/iframe injections, BSS B2B JS injecting elements, hover sequences. DO NOT lab-guess; use the real data we have.

title: Vertex AI Search for Commerce — replace LimeSpot (4-6 week build, separate session)
description: Replace LimeSpot ($4,836/yr) with Google Vertex AI Search for Commerce (~$50-150/mo + $600 free credit covers first 6mo). Net savings $3,000-4,200/yr. Direct replacement, no A/B test (decided 2026-04-27). Build phases: (1) GCP setup + billing + service accounts. (2) Catalog sync (Cloud Function: Shopify products + brand metafields + per-brand profit margins → Vertex catalog, daily + webhook). (3) Event tracking (page views, add-to-cart, purchase → Vertex events, real-time, deferred analytics). (4) Server-rendered Liquid widgets on Homepage / PDP (FBT + YMAL) / Cart (Complete the Routine). Profit re-rank in Liquid: score × (1 + brand_margin_pct/100). (5) Cutover: disable LimeSpot widgets, ship Vertex, monitor RPV/AOV/CVR/mobile-TBT 14 days, instant rollback by re-enabling LimeSpot. (6) After 14 clean days, uninstall LimeSpot. Performance non-negotiables: server-side render (zero JS to display), <100ms p95 predict latency via caching, <50ms TBT impact, mobile TBT lower than LimeSpot baseline. Build in a fresh Claude Code session with the prompt already prepared.

title: Founder decision: full uninstall of BSS B2B Customer Portal
description: JS already gated to logged-in pros only via customer.tags contains 'backbar-approved' (~185KB saved per guest pageload). Open question for founders: do we still need the BSS B2B Customer Portal app for the pro accounts? If usage is low, full uninstall captures the monthly fee. Cost: TBD. Verify pro account activity before deciding.

title: Manually delete orphan BSS Product Labels asset files from Shopify admin
description: BSS Product Labels theme code already removed (commits 0fa3f63 + e3644bd) but the asset files remain in the Shopify admin assets folder as orphans. Need to manually delete via Shopify admin → Online Store → Themes → Edit code → assets → find bss-product-labels-* files and delete each.

title: Remove Reddit Pixel via GTM admin if Reddit ads not driving conversions
description: Lighthouse measured 128ms forced reflow from redditstatic.com/ads/pixel.js. Loaded dynamically by GTM (initiator=script in Performance API), NOT in theme code. Marketing decision: pause/delete the Reddit Pixel tag in GTM admin if Reddit ads are not earning their slot. Small win (~128ms TBT + one fewer third-party tracker).
close_reason: User confirmed 2026-04-27 EOD: Reddit destination turned off in Elevar admin. Saves ~128 ms forced reflow + removes a third-party tracker. No code change needed; Elevar admin toggle handled it.

title: Verify Subscribe It, Treedify, Hextom CTB install status — remove dead theme code
description: Apps appear uninstalled but theme snippets still rendering (similar to Smile / AAA / GSF cases already cleaned). Web team check Shopify admin → Apps. If uninstalled: remove the includes + delete snippets. (a) Subscribe It — snippets/subscribe-it-helper.liquid (54KB / 178 lines / 2 scripts — biggest win). (b) Treedify — snippets/treedify.liquid + snippets/treedify_script.liquid (1.6KB / 4 scripts). (c) Hextom CTB — snippets/hextom_ctb_main.liquid (1.2KB / 1 script).

title: Judge.me admin: enable Lite mode + reduce reviews-per-page (web team)
description: Diagnosed as dominant mobile TBT blocker (~5,000ms script eval on widget_*.js files). Hand off to web team for in-app optimization. Steps: Judge.me admin → Settings → Customization → enable Lite/Performance mode if available; disable unused widgets (Q&A, Photo Reviews, FAQ); reduce reviews-per-page to 5-10 with pagination; limit which page templates show widgets. If TBT contribution still >1500ms after these tweaks, escalate to Judge.me support requesting defer/lazy options.

title: Lazy-init .homepage-blog setMaxHeight via IntersectionObserver
description: article-card.js inside custom-theme.js (line ~14238) runs setTimeout(setMaxHeight, 100ms) on .homepage-blog containers at DOMContentLoaded. setMaxHeight does layout reads (clientHeight) + writes (style.height) on each .article__card title element — classic layout thrash. On homepages where the blog section is below fold, this runs as a DCL-time burst for content the user hasn't scrolled to. Solution: same IntersectionObserver pattern as ua7 — observe .homepage-blog elements with rootMargin: '200px' and only run setMaxHeight when the section is about to enter viewport. Estimated 50-150ms TBT reduction (smaller than ua7 because the work is lighter, but still a clean win).
close_reason: IntersectionObserver wrapper around setMaxHeight; 200px rootMargin; preserves 100ms settle delay. Pushed and committed.

title: Re-introduce brand-strip on /collections/<brand>/* via gated strip_brand param
description: Earlier brand-strip fix (commit reverted 2026-04-29) attempted to remove redundant brand prefix on Kérastase / Davines / etc. landing pages. Was applied unconditionally to product-grid-item.liquid and broke ALL product cards site-wide. To re-do safely: add strip_brand:true param to the render calls in collection-branded.liquid, collection-lorealprofessionnel.liquid, collection-discipline.liquid, collection-resistance.liquid, collection-nutritive.liquid, collection-krsymbiose.liquid, collection-krchromaabsolu.liquid, collection-lp-metaldetox.liquid, collection-lp-styling.liquid (~9 branded sections). Then in product-grid-item.liquid, gate the replace chain behind {% if strip_brand %}. Test: brand visible on /, /collections/all, /search; brand stripped on /collections/davines, /collections/kerastase, etc.
close_reason: strip_brand param added to product-grid-item.liquid; collection-branded.liquid + collection-lorealprofessionnel.liquid pass strip_brand: true on the swiper-slide renders. Pushed and committed.

title: Image asset audit: 782 KiB savings on azta-urban gallery JPGs (oversized)
description:
2026-04-28 PSI image-delivery-insight on /pages/azta-urban-salon-service-menu showed 782 KiB of wasted bytes across multiple Azta service-menu gallery JPGs. Top offenders:

  135 KB wasted: 2020_Azta_General_Service_Menu-22_1080x.jpg
   60 KB wasted: 2020_Azta_General_Service_Menu-21_1080x.jpg
   58 KB wasted: 2020_Azta_General_Service_Menu-04_1080x.jpg
   56 KB wasted: 2020_Azta_General_Service_Menu-13_1080x.jpg
   52 KB wasted: 2020_Azta_General_Service_Menu-05_1080x.jpg
  ... (more)

These are 1080x JPGs being delivered to mobile viewports that only need ~720x. Two fixable angles:

1. (theme-side) Add WebP variant via image_url with format: 'webp' filter. WebP is 30-50% smaller than JPG at equivalent quality. We've shipped this pattern for product images via <picture> wrap; needs same treatment in image-fill.liquid.
2. (admin-side) Re-export source JPGs at smaller dimensions / better compression. The Shopify CDN serves whatever was uploaded — if source is huge, even the resized variants are larger than needed.

Worth ~2 hr for theme-side WebP wrap in image-fill.liquid (high impact: every section using image-fill benefits). Plus ~30 min admin task to flag the worst source JPGs for re-export.

title: /account page CLS+LCP+FCP+TTFB all in poor — server-side + app-injection issues
description:
Real-shopper RUM data 2026-04-28 shows /account page failing 4 of 5 Core Web Vitals (only INP passes):
- TTFB: 30% good (Shopify customer-area auth-check + customer.orders DB query — server-side, hard to fix from theme)
- FCP: 36% good
- LCP: 44% good (well below 75% CrUX threshold)
- CLS: 35% good
- INP: 96% good ✓

Template code (templates/customers/account.liquid, 118 lines) is clean — just orders table.

Root causes (theme can't fix all, but can mitigate):
1. Shopify customer-area pages aren't CDN-cached (auth required) → slow TTFB inherent
2. LimeSpot / LoyaltyLion / Klaviyo activate fully on logged-in pages → heavy JS
3. PWA modal injection (separate beads — see #pwa-modal CSS fix)
4. customer.orders query renders inline → LCP delays

Theme-side actions to try:
1. Conditionally skip non-essential apps on /account templates (LimeSpot recs, etc — they don't drive customer-area conversion anyway)
2. Minimize CSS loaded on customer-area templates (per-template CSS already split, but customer-area might pull more than needed)
3. Investigate if Klaviyo signup modal fires on /account (often does) — it shouldn't

Not worth a major code investment until other fixes shipped — /account is low-traffic (216 events / 2 days vs homepage's 1,617). But it's the only page with TTFB <50% so worth a dedicated 1-2 hr session.
close_reason: Theme-side mitigation shipped (commit a8407fc): LimeSpot SDK now skipped on customers/* templates — saves ~300KB JS + DNS + parse on every customer-area page request. The remaining TTFB / LCP issues are server-side (Shopify customer-area auth-check, customer.orders DB query) — unfixable from theme code. Other app-skip candidates (VQB, Klaviyo signup modal, Judge.me) noted in commit message for future iteration. Re-open if RUM after 14d shows /account still failing 4+ metrics.

title: Snippet DRY-up: consolidate 3 product-grid-item variants (~600 lines duplicated)
description:
Three nearly-identical files with significant code duplication:
  - snippets/product-grid-item.liquid (standard, used by most collections)
  - snippets/product-grid-item-branded.liquid (used by branded collection landing pages)
  - snippets/product-grid-item-branded-subcollection.liquid (used by sub-sections of branded collections)

All three render product cards with: WCProduct_Price logic (Wholesale Club), WebP <picture> wrap, brand-prefix strip from title, sticker (sale/sold/new), price + compare-at price, Judge.me badge.

Differences between the three:
  - branded variants strip brand prefix more aggressively (Davines/Aveda/etc whole word matches)
  - branded-subcollection has narrower image sizes and tighter padding
  - standard has hover-second-image swap; branded does not

Proposed consolidation:
1. Rename product-grid-item.liquid → product-card.liquid (new canonical).
2. Add params: variant ('standard'|'branded'|'branded-subcollection'), strip_brand_prefix (bool), show_hover_image (bool), image_sizes (string).
3. Branch internally based on params.
4. Replace all callers — sweep snippets/sections for {% render 'product-grid-item-*' %} and migrate to {% render 'product-card', variant: '...' %}.
5. Keep the old files as { include 'product-card', variant: 'XXX' } for one release cycle, then delete.

Risk: high regression risk on collection / branded-collection pages. Need to test rendering on:
  - /collections/all (standard)
  - /collections/davines (branded)
  - /collections/loreal-professionnel (branded sub-collections)
  - homepage product grids

Worth 3-4 hours: 1 hr to consolidate, 1 hr to migrate callers, 1-2 hr to test all variants. High maintenance value (any future product card change touches 1 file instead of 3) but no shopper-facing impact.

title: Critical CSS audit + regeneration: ~40KB inline contains 37 Swiper + 10 Flickity selectors, some duplicates
description:
2026-04-28 spot audit of snippets/critical-css.liquid (39,927 bytes, single-line minified, inlined in <head> per layout/theme.liquid:17).

Findings:
  - 37 Swiper selectors (.swiper-button-*, .swiper-slide, etc) — used by homepage banner-slider (above-the-fold on homepage). JUSTIFIED.
  - 10 Flickity selectors (.flickity-*) — used by PDP product slideshow (NOT above-the-fold on homepage). Could potentially be moved out of critical CSS for non-PDP pages.
  - At least 5 duplicate rules detected (e.g., .uppercase, .js-grid[data-grid-small=2], 3x .collection__filters__*).
  - Many template-specific rules (.tousled-header__*) that may not be above-the-fold on every template.

Manual pruning is risky — could remove a rule that IS critical on some template. Better approach: regenerate critical CSS via tooling.

Recommended path:
1. Install  npm package (https://github.com/addyosmani/critical) or  (https://github.com/pocketjoso/penthouse).
2. Run against a representative set of templates (homepage, PDP davines-momo, collection davines, /cart, /pages/about, blog article).
3. Diff output vs current critical-css.liquid — identify rules to remove (or keep).
4. Manually merge: union of all per-template critical CSS becomes the new shared critical CSS.
5. Likely savings: 10-30% reduction (~4-12 KB) based on the Flickity + duplicates findings.

Alternative: ship the current critical CSS as-is and instead split into per-template inline blocks (e.g., critical-css-pdp.liquid, critical-css-collection.liquid) so each page only inlines what it needs. More code change but more savings.

Worth ~3-4 hr for proper audit + tooling setup + regeneration.

title: A11y follow-ups: button-name, color-contrast, label-content-name-mismatch (3 Lighthouse audits still failing)
description:
2026-04-28 Lighthouse a11y audit found 4 failures. ONE fixed in this commit (aria-allowed-attr — footer accordion

→

title: Audit + remove ~15 likely-orphan snippets (icons, BSS variants, oxi-social, membership-product-callbacks)
description:
2026-04-28 orphan snippet sweep found these candidates (not referenced via {% render %} or {% include %} anywhere in snippets/sections/layout/templates):

DELETED in this commit (high confidence):
- snippets/loyaltylion copy.liquid (duplicate file with ' copy' suffix)
- snippets/judgeme_core.liquid (disabled 2026-04-26 — Judge.me app auto-injects identical JS via content_for_header per layout/theme.liquid:430-438 comment)
- snippets/esf-rem-slash.liquid (no internal references)

NEEDS RESEARCH (could be conditionally rendered or app-injected):
- snippets/cross-post-blogs.liquid — was edited today for alt fix; might be app-injected via content_for_header from a blog cross-post app
- snippets/oxi-social-login.liquid — Oxi social login app, possibly removed
- snippets/membership-product-callbacks.liquid — possibly removed app
- snippets/icon-arrow-left.liquid + icon-arrow-right.liquid + icon-plus.liquid — might be referenced via Liquid {{ 'arrow-left' | render_icon }} pattern that grep doesn't catch
- ~25 BSS B2B / BSS Commerce snippets — most are app-injected via /admin and content_for_header; only safe to remove after BSS uninstall (see beads bjk for that decision)
  - bss-b2b-collection-item-vat-styles
  - bss-b2b-collection-item-vat
  - bss-b2b-country
  - bss-b2b-cp-header / cp-rules-data
  - bss-b2b-discount-code-form
  - bss-b2b-header
  - bss-b2b-pl-rules-data
  - bss-b2b-product-vat
  - bss-b2b-state-config
  - bss-b2b-tax-cart-item / line-item / styles / subtotal
  - bss-b2b-tax-exempt
  - bss-custom-login
  - bss-ltap-fl-redirect
  - bss-ltap-hv-js / hv-rules-logic
  - bss-ltap-passcode-form / ptvp-passcode-form
  - bss-passcode-to-see-price-js
  - bsscommerce-* (10+ variants)

For each NEEDS RESEARCH item: open the file → check if it has a clear caller pattern → check Shopify app installation status → safe-to-delete only if both 'no caller' AND 'app uninstalled'.

Follow-up to dead-snippet sweep work. Bundled as one P3 task because each requires per-snippet judgment.

title: Image alt audit: 6 branded collection sections + cross-post-blogs missing alts
description: 2026-04-28 alt audit found these images without alt attributes: FIXED in this commit: - snippets/cross-post-blogs.liquid:15 — added alt={article.title} STILL MISSING (filed as follow-up): - sections/collection-krchromaabsolu.liquid (lines 347, 350, 362, 371, 380, 394, 403, 412) - sections/collection-krsymbiose.liquid (similar block patterns) - sections/collection-lp-vitamino.liquid (lines 346, 349) - sections/collection-lp-metaldetox.liquid (lines 347, 350) - sections/collection-lp-serioxyladvanced.liquid (line 346, others) - sections/collection-lorealprofessionnel.liquid (lines 410, 438) These are branded landing page sections rendering imgs from block.settings.left_media_html / middle_media_html / right_media_html / product_image_1/2/3. The img source is a raw URL (settings field), not a Shopify Image object, so there's no .alt attribute to read. Fix options: 1. (Best) Add alt-text fields to each block schema (block.settings.image_alt / left_media_alt / etc), wire into render 2. Use the block's title/heading text as alt where present 3. Add a generic alt like 'L\'Oreal Professionnel Vitamino' for the section's branded products Worth ~1-2 hr per section family, ~6 hrs total for thorough fix. PRE-EXISTING empty alts (NOT bugs — likely intentional decorative): - sections/password.liquid:20, 24 — password page background images, decorative - sections/section-map.liquid:64 — map decorative Leaving those as-is (empty alt is the correct WCAG pattern for decorative images).

title: Cart drawer skeleton-state delay: AJAX cart populate takes several seconds
description:
After opening the cart drawer, the skeleton placeholder (data-cart-loading + cart__items--blankstate) stays visible for several seconds before items populate. User reports 'froze for several seconds'.

Suspected culprits (in approximate order of likely impact):
1. /cart.js (or section_id=api-cart) endpoint slow due to app overhead — BSS B2B, Bold Brain, Smart SEO, Judge.me, Elevar all hook into cart rendering
2. AJAX request not fired until drawer opens (could pre-fetch on page load idle)
3. Cart drawer rerendering pulls a Liquid section that re-runs all the cart-related app injections each time

Diagnostic steps:
1. Open DevTools Network tab, click cart icon, measure /cart.js timing
2. Check what section_id the drawer fetches (likely api-cart or api-product-popdown)
3. Profile JS execution after items arrive (skeleton-to-real swap performance)

Quick wins:
- Pre-fetch cart on requestIdleCallback (drawer is then instant when user clicks)
- Cache the rendered cart HTML in sessionStorage; refresh on add/remove
- Strip out app injections from the drawer-specific Liquid section (drawer doesn't need full /cart page hydration)

Worth ~30-60 min investigation slot.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 'fix(cart-drawer): kill 4 residual bugs surfaced by tonight's CSS hotfix'. All four shipped to live theme 131664707683 on 2026-04-28 night. Verified on draft 140785582179 before live push.

title: Header cart icon: defer-load JS race lets click fall through to /cart link before drawer JS binds
description:
On desktop, clicking the cart icon sometimes navigates to /cart standalone page instead of opening the slide-out drawer. On narrower viewports it works correctly.

Root cause: header cart icons (sections/header.liquid lines 134-140 mobile + 189-204 desktop) have BOTH:
  - href={{ routes.cart_url }} (link to /cart)
  - data-drawer-toggle=drawer-cart (JS hook)

The Drawer.connectToggle() handler in theme.js:1980-1994 does e.preventDefault() to suppress link navigation, but only AFTER theme.js loads. theme.js is loaded with defer (theme.liquid:378), so on a fast cold-cache page, the user can click the cart icon BEFORE the deferred script finishes parsing — link navigation wins.

Worsened by 2026-04-27 perf work: jquery, theme.js, storepickup.js all deferred. Gap between paint and JS-ready can be 200-500ms on a fast Mac.

Fix options:
1. Inline a tiny event-delegation script in <head> that listens at document level for clicks on [data-drawer-toggle] and preventDefault until the real handler binds. Hand off after.
2. Remove the href from the desktop cart icon (loses fallback for JS-disabled users; rare).
3. Add a CSS class to <html> when JS is ready, conditionally hide the link's pointer-events until then (jank).
4. Use JS module preload + inline an early data-drawer-toggle stub.

Option 1 is cheapest and safest. ~10 lines of inline JS in <head>.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 'fix(cart-drawer): kill 4 residual bugs surfaced by tonight's CSS hotfix'. All four shipped to live theme 131664707683 on 2026-04-28 night. Verified on draft 140785582179 before live push.

title: Cart drawer: clamp 'Spend ₱X more' to ₱0 when cart already qualifies for free shipping
close_reason: Fixed in commit 'fix(cart-drawer): kill 4 residual bugs surfaced by tonight's CSS hotfix'. All four shipped to live theme 131664707683 on 2026-04-28 night. Verified on draft 140785582179 before live push.

title: Split cart.css into cart-shared.css (drawer-shared) + cart-page.css (page-only)
description:
Today (2026-04-27) we discovered that commit 949888a extracted .cart__message + .cart__graph (free-shipping promo + progress dots) from theme.css into cart.css under the assumption they were cart-page-only. They aren't — they're also used by the slide-out cart drawer (snippets/cart-drawer.liquid), which appears on PDP/collection/header/every page. Commit a57b0b8 then gated cart.css to template == 'cart', breaking the drawer's promo banner layout sitewide.

Hotfix tonight: removed the gate so cart.css loads on all pages (~3KB gzipped extra cost on non-cart pages, async, no render blocking).

Proper fix: split cart.css (~9KB / 465 lines) into:
  - cart-shared.css (~5KB, lines ~8-200): .cart__message + .cart__graph and all variants. Used by both /cart page and drawer. Load on all templates.
  - cart-page.css (~4KB, lines ~3, 297-465): .cart__template + .template__cart__footer rules. Only used by /cart page. Gate to template == 'cart'.

Saves ~3KB gzipped on non-cart pages and restores the cart-page-only gating for the cart-page-only chunk.
close_reason: Split shipped 2026-04-28. cart.css trimmed from 465 to 199 lines (shared rules only). New cart-page.css with 262 page-only lines, gated to template == 'cart'. Saves ~1.4KB gzipped on every non-cart page. Verified on draft via Brave inspection — /cart loads both files, PDP loads only cart.css, drawer .cart__message correctly styled display:flex on both.

title: Re-run PSI tomorrow morning to verify CDN+Klaviyo cache fully settled
description: Verification baseline. Today's wins shipped within last few hours: BSS B2B JS gating, lazysizes purge, font self-host, Klaviyo defer, AAA/GSF dead-code purge, CSS bundle deferral rounds 1-4. Latest PSI screenshot (Apr 27 9:42 GMT+2) showed mobile score 36 (+10), LCP 6.3s (-50%), TBT 1530ms (-65%) vs EOD-26 baseline. Re-run 2-3x tomorrow to confirm representative not lucky-run. Document any regression. CrUX field will not move until ~mid-May (28-day rolling).

title: Performance dashboard finalization (Page 3 + Page 2 Table 3)
description: User finishing manual layout. Page 3: swap WoW scorecard filters to INP/CLS, add 90-day targets text block, rename page. Page 2 Table 3 (Top CLS targets): verify Event=CLS filter is set. Once 24-48h GA4 data flows: review actual baselines, prioritize next phase from real data not CrUX-predicted.

title: WebP image rollout — biggest remaining mobile bandwidth lever (parked)
description: Lighthouse 'Avoid enormous network payloads — 7,567 KiB' is the next big lever. WebP is 30-50% smaller than JPEG. Pilot on banner-slider failed: image_url filter with format:'webp' silently drops <source type="image/webp"> when applied to block.settings.image_picker (Pipeline 6.1.3 returns URL string, not Image object). Approaches to try in a focused future session: (1) capture image_picker into variable, re-parse as Image object. (2) test if older img_url filter accepts format: param. (3) self-host pre-converted WebP variants. (4) wait for Shopify CDN native Accept-header negotiation. Files using product.featured_image (real Image object) like snippets/product-grid-item-branded.liquid are likely to work — pilot there first.
close_reason: WebP rollout shipped to live 2026-04-27. <picture> + <source type='image/webp'> wrap added to product-grid-item.liquid (active) and product-grid-item-branded.liquid (dormant variant for consistency). Verified on draft /collections/loreal-professionnel: 363 <picture> blocks, 363 image/webp source lines, 1,330 format=webp URL params. Pipeline 6.1.3 image_url filter works after all — earlier attempts failed because they piped .src (URL string) instead of the raw Image object. Estimated 30-50% image bytes saved on collection pages.

title: Cleanup: delete 4 orphan image assets from Shopify admin (AMP logos + wsgLoadingIcon.gif)
description:
2026-04-28 image asset audit found 4 unreferenced images in /assets/. Deleted from local repo. Manual deletion from Shopify admin needed to free storage:

  - wsgLoadingIcon.gif (45,800 bytes — possibly Wishlist/legacy loading spinner, no theme references)
  - amp-1594739828536-HairMNLVerticalBlack.png (22,697 bytes)
  - amp-1594734639365-HairMNLHorizontalBlack.png (20,917 bytes)
  - amp-1594653446665-onboarding-uploaded-logo.png (20,917 bytes)

The 3 amp-* files are AMP page logos (Shop Sheriff AMP app). AMP is being sunset (see beads osj). Even before app uninstall, these images are unreferenced by theme code (Google may still serve them via cached AMP pages until the 'mobile_link' attribute drops from Search Console).

Manual cleanup: Shopify admin → Themes → Live theme → Edit code → assets/ → search and delete each filename.

5 min admin task. Frees ~110 KB Shopify-side asset storage.

title: Cleanup: delete legacy .css.liquid files from Shopify admin (problogger.css.liquid, problogger-custom.css.liquid)
description:
2026-04-28 — renamed assets/problogger.css.liquid → assets/problogger.css (and .custom variant) since both have ZERO Liquid templating tags. The new .css files were pushed to Shopify; references via {{ 'problogger.css' | asset_url }} resolve correctly (Shopify finds .css in preference to .css.liquid when both exist). The OLD .css.liquid files remain on Shopify as orphans — references in code now point to the new .css versions, but the old files still take up Shopify-side asset storage.

Manual cleanup needed via Shopify admin → Themes → Live theme → Edit code → assets/ → delete:
  - problogger.css.liquid
  - problogger-custom.css.liquid
  - quick-stylesheet.css.liquid (was empty file, deleted locally)

5 min admin task. No risk if done after live push of new .css versions has been verified.

title: use_custom_accent_font theme setting is vestigial — toggle exists but never gates custom_accent_font
description:
2026-04-28 theme settings audit found: settings_schema.json defines a checkbox 'use_custom_accent_font' but no Liquid template references it. The actual font selector 'custom_accent_font' (a select) IS referenced in snippets/css-variables.liquid:89-90 — it's applied unconditionally when set, not gated by the toggle.

Fix options:
1. Remove use_custom_accent_font from settings_schema.json (clears merchant UI).
2. Or wire it up: change css-variables.liquid:89 to: if settings.use_custom_accent_font and settings.custom_accent_font (gated).

Option 2 is more user-friendly to merchants (toggle on/off without losing the saved font choice). ~10 min change. Worth verifying which merchants currently have the toggle in different states before rolling out (might cause a visual change for some).

title: BOGOS replacement: build custom buy-X-get-Y promo logic (1 day, $420/yr saved)
description: BOGOS app handles buy-one-get-one promotions. Custom build: detect promo-eligible items in cart via Liquid + auto-add free item via Shopify cart AJAX API. ~1 day of theme work. Saves $420/yr in subscription. Lower priority than larger savings; only do if BOGOS promo behavior is well-understood + stable.
notes: Folded into r8r (STKY replacement) scope per shared codebase. The free-gift threshold meter in r8r Phase 2.2 handles BOGOS use case (auto-add free product when cart > threshold, auto-remove if drops below). Combined replacement saves $720/yr in 5-6 hrs. See memory/stky_replacement_scope.md.

title: STKY Add to Cart: build custom replacement (3-4 hrs, $300/yr saved)
description: Sticky add-to-cart on PDP currently provided by an app. Custom build: position:fixed bottom bar that appears when product Add-to-Cart button scrolls out of viewport (IntersectionObserver), with current variant + quantity + Add button. ~3-4 hrs of theme work. Saves $300/yr in subscription. Lower priority than the bigger app rationalizations (LimeSpot, AMP) but easy win when capacity allows.
notes: Comprehensive scope written 2026-04-27 EOD: memory/stky_replacement_scope.md. Scope mirrors the Vertex AI replacement pattern (server-side first, JS-minimal). Phase 1 = STKY parity (~2 hrs). Phase 2 = the 'more features' delta — free-shipping meter, free-gift meter (also covers BOGOS replacement, ur0), quick-add chips, cart-count badge, Shop Pay, low-stock indicator, Elevar event integration (~3-4 hrs). Phase 2.5 = cross-sell via Vertex AI (deferred until Vertex ships). Total active dev: ~5-6 hrs combined STKY+BOGOS replacement → $720/yr saved. Slot suggested: early-to-mid May 2026 after Phase 2 perf work, before Vertex build kicks off. Build prompt embedded in the scope doc for the future Claude Code session.

title: Custom-theme.js layout-thrash audit (no sourcemaps, deferred)
description: Lighthouse Performance trace showed forced-reflow events in custom-theme.js. No sourcemaps shipped to prod = hard to map back to which source line caused which offsetWidth/offsetHeight read. Approach options: (1) add sourcemaps temporarily for one PSI run. (2) inspect bundled JS by hand and look for forceReflow patterns. (3) use Chrome DevTools Performance tab on a real page interaction. Deferred until other Phase 2 items shipped.

title: jQuery removal — multi-day project, high risk (parked)
description: jQuery still loaded sitewide. Removing it would cut ~80KB but requires rewriting any theme code that uses $.foo, $.ajax, etc. plus auditing all third-party JS that depends on jQuery being available globally (announcement bar, popups, some app scripts). High risk of breaking things mid-flight. Multi-day focused session if attempted.

value: Draft theme drift cleanup 2026-04-27: pushed 134+ files from main to draft 140785582179. Shopify CLI bulk push proven inconsistent (success message often lies; pull --nodelete also flaky — repeated pulls return stale data). Solution: push individually OR in small batches and verify via live HTML curl markers, not via repeat pull. Vertex preview files (5 sections + 3 snippets) and limespot-prefix-strip.liquid preserved on draft (truly pending experiments). Dead app code remaining on draft for manual deletion via Shopify admin: bss-product-label-* (4 assets + 3 snippets), aaa-announcementbar-configuration, gsf-conversion-pixels, shop-sheriff-amp, shop-sheriff-pwa, smile-initializer, booster_eu_cookie. 55 legacy promo PNG files with broken _png extensions can't be synced via CLI (Shopify rejects unrecognized extension). Lesson: any future bulk theme sync should script per-file pushes with retry, not rely on bulk --only batches.

Strategic decision 2026-04-27: merge perf/judgeme-defer branch (Judge.me static stars + deferred loader) — work was 90% done, real LCP/TBT win, low marginal cost. AFTER this, NO MORE theme-side Judge.me work. Residual perf cost goes to web-team admin defer (issue ebh: Lite mode, reduce reviews-per-page, disable unused widgets) — that path uses Judge.me's own settings without adding theme-side vendor coupling. Custom metafield parser is acceptable maintenance liability for one branch but compounds quickly if extended. If Judge.me changes badge HTML format and our Liquid split breaks, revert this branch — graceful fallback to loader.js hydration is the rollback path. Don't replace Judge.me (/mo too cheap to justify churn — Vertex AI replacement scope is LimeSpot only).

Judge.me loader.js v490 (fetched 2026-04-27 from cdn.shopify.com/extensions/.../judgeme-490/assets/loader.js, 9926 bytes) maps widget JS files to CSS gating selectors. Critical entry: widget/main.js is gated on '.jdgm-preview-badge, .judgeme-preview-badge' (full word, NOT the short .jdgm-prev-badge). This means renaming Liquid wrapper class from full to short form bypasses badge hydration entirely. Document this if Judge.me ever updates the loader — class names or gating logic could shift and break our static-stars implementation.

HairMNL uses LoyaltyLion for the loyalty program, NOT Smile.io. Earlier session notes / my own assumptions referenced Smile — incorrect. When auditing vendor JS / TBT contributors / admin-side perf work, the loyalty vendor to look for is LoyaltyLion (commonly: loyaltylion.com / cdn.loyaltylion.net / loyaltylion-launcher / data-lion-rewards-iframe / window.loyaltylion). Any future 'reduce loyalty JS' task targets LoyaltyLion's settings / launcher placement, not Smile.
